request.description: Replaces the User at the specified URL with a new User item, using the JSON object in the request body. NOTE: This method completely replaces the entire object, previous field values are not retained. If you only want to update certain fields (e.g. the User's Name or Permissions field only), see the Patch User example request.

exec: throw new Error('user_id not set, try running the Create User request first');

request.description: Patches/modifies the User item at the specified URL, using the field values in the JSON request body. Only the field values specified in the body will be applied to the object, other fields will remain unmodified. If you wish to fully replace a User item, see the Update (Replace) User example request.

request.description: Deletes the User at the specified URL, erasing it from the Inception database if it exists. Returns a blank 200 OK response if successful.

request.description:
Virtually badges card data at a reader to simulate a user physically badging a card.

Use the "Control/Doors/Get Door's Associated Readers" request to fetch a reader's ID, and put it in the "reader_id" environment variable.

Use the "Configuration/Get All Card Templates" request to fetch a credential template ID, and put it in the "credentialtemplate_id" environment variable.

Edit the "user_cardnumber" environment variable to set the card number.

exec: throw new Error('reader_id not set, try setting the environment variable manually');

request.description:
Virtually badges card data at a reader to simulate a user physically badging a card.

Use the "Control/Doors/Get Door's Associated Readers" request to fetch a reader's ID, and put it in the "reader_id" environment variable.

Edit the "user_pin" environment variable to set the PIN data.

request.description: This request manually fetches the newest review event in the system to use as a reference point (via setting environment variables) so that we can poll for events that are newer than this one.
